title: MIPPE Scale Validation Protocol
author: Emmanuelle Dufait
materials: Paramedical professionals (speech therapists, educators, psychomotor therapists)
Read the MIPPE scale presentation and scoring instructions
Rated each video using the 9-item MIPPE scale

steps.title: Preliminary Rating of the Full Video Corpus
steps.instruction: Preliminary Rating of the Full Video Corpus: The lead researcher (E. Dufait) rated all 228 PERL videos using the MIPPE scale. This preliminary rating step served two purposes: (1) familiarization with item functioning and identification of potential ambiguities leading to final scale refinements; (2) classification of videos into three quality categories ('good', 'average', 'poor' interaction) to guide the selection of representative videos for the psychometric validation sample.

steps.title: 2 videos rated as 'good' interaction...
steps.instruction: 2 videos rated as 'good' interaction (1 at 4 months, 1 at 24 months)

steps.title: Selection criteria included representativeness of interaction...
steps.instruction: Selection criteria included representativeness of interaction quality levels, video technical quality (image stability, audio clarity), and diversity of caregiver-child dyad characteristics.

steps.title: Recruitment of Professional Raters
steps.instruction: Recruitment of Professional Raters: Early childhood professionals were recruited through professional networks, institutional contacts, and social media channels targeting the following socio-professional categories:

steps.title: Physicians (psychiatrists, pediatricians, child psychiatrists, general practitioners, PMI physicians, midwives)
steps.instruction: Physicians (psychiatrists, pediatricians, child psychiatrists, general practitioners, PMI physicians, midwives)

steps.title: Nurses and child nurses (nurses, psychiatric...
steps.instruction: Nurses and child nurses (nurses, psychiatric nurses, pediatric nurses, advanced practice nurses, child nurses)

steps.title: Recruitment targeted a minimum of 100...
steps.instruction: Recruitment targeted a minimum of 100 raters to ensure adequate statistical power for factor analysis and reliability estimation, consistent with established guidelines for scale validation (5–10 participants per item minimum, with a minimum of 100 total).

steps.title: Rating Procedure via Online Platform
steps.instruction: Rating Procedure via Online Platform: Each participant was directed to the dedicated online platform via the unique URL shared through professional networks. Upon accessing the platform, participants:

steps.title: Were randomly assigned one panel of...
steps.instruction: Were randomly assigned one panel of 6 videos (all at 4 months, representing good, average, and poor interaction quality)

steps.title: Provided sociodemographic information (profession, years of...
steps.instruction: Provided sociodemographic information (profession, years of experience, type of clinical population)

steps.title: Each rater assessed the same panel of 6 videos
steps.instruction: Each rater assessed the same panel of 6 videos. Participation was entirely voluntary. No financial compensation was provided. Anonymity was guaranteed throughout the data collection process.

steps.title: Data Collection and Management
steps.instruction: Data Collection and Management: Rating data were transmitted directly from the online platform to the research team's secure email. Data were then entered into a structured database and pseudonymized. No nominative data were retained. Data from raters who did not complete all 6 ratings were excluded from analysis.

steps.title: Final sample
steps.instruction: Final sample: N = 143 raters who provided complete ratings across all 6 videos, yielding 858 individual video ratings (143 × 6).

steps.title: Descriptive Analyses
steps.instruction: Descriptive Analyses: Distribution of responses for each MIPPE item across the 143 raters, tests of normality (Shapiro-Wilk) at item level and total score level, homogeneity of variances (Levene's test).

steps.title: Content Validity — Internal Consistency
steps.instruction: Content Validity — Internal Consistency: Cronbach's alpha (target: α �3e 0.70), McDonald's omega (more appropriate for non-normal Likert data), corrected item-total correlations (threshold: r �3e 0.30).

steps.title: Construct Validity — Factor Structure
steps.instruction: Construct Validity — Factor Structure: Exploratory Factor Analysis (EFA) performed using a polychoric correlation matrix (appropriate for ordinal Likert data); Kaiser-Meyer-Olkin (KMO) index and Bartlett's test of sphericity to assess sampling adequacy; eigenvalue �3e 1 criterion for factor retention.

steps.title: Confirmatory Factor Analysis (CFA)
steps.instruction: Confirmatory Factor Analysis (CFA): tested using lavaan package with DWLS estimator; fit indices evaluated: CFI (target ≥ 0.95), TLI (target ≥ 0.95), RMSEA (target ≤ 0.06), SRMR (target ≤ 0.08), chi-square p-value.

steps.title: Discriminant Validity
steps.instruction: Discriminant Validity: Two-factor ANOVA (socio-professional category × interaction quality) to assess whether MIPPE scores differ across professional backgrounds. Expected result: significant effect of interaction quality (p �3c 0.05), non-significant effect of professional category (confirming cross-disciplinary usability). Levene's test for homogeneity of variances between professional groups. Seniority analysis: comparison of MIPPE scores across experience levels (threshold: 11 years).

steps.title: Convergent and Divergent Validity
steps.instruction: Convergent and Divergent Validity: Convergent: Pearson correlations between MIPPE total scores and: maternal education level, 'desired child' variable, Revised Brunet-Lézine developmental scale (language subscale), ADBB (alarm détresse bébé) scale — assessed at 4 months (N = 121) and 24 months (N = 107). Divergent: correlation between MIPPE scores and presence of addiction in the family (expected: negative correlation).

steps.title: Clinical Threshold Determination
steps.instruction: Clinical Threshold Determination: Comparison of multiple threshold combinations to optimize classification into three quality categories (poor / average / good). Optimization criteria: sensitivity (prioritized), specificity, and overall accuracy. Final thresholds: ≤ 15 (poor interaction, intervention recommended); 16–20 (average, monitoring recommended); �3e 20 (good quality).

steps.title: The original PERL research protocol received...
steps.instruction: The original PERL research protocol received ethics approval from the Committee for the Protection of Persons North-West IV (CPP Nord-Ouest IV) on November 15, 2017 (reference IDRCB: 2017-A00896-47), with updated approval for substantial amendment N°7 on August 24, 2023. The PERL study also received authorization from the French National Commission for Information Technology and Civil Liberties (CNIL, Decision DR-2018-10, January 30, 2018). All parent participants provided written informed consent prior to inclusion, including specific authorization for video recording and use for research, educational, and scientific presentation purposes. Professional raters provided implicit informed consent through voluntary access to the online platform. Rater data were fully anonymous.

steps.title: A validated 9-item observational scale for...
steps.instruction: A validated 9-item observational scale for early parent-child interaction assessment (ages 4–24 months) Unidimensional factorial structure with acceptable to excellent fit indices High internal consistency (Cronbach's α ≥ 0.80) Discriminant validity across 5 professional categories (no significant effect of profession on scores) Convergent validity with established developmental and relational measures Clinically usable thresholds for triaging interaction quality

description: Selection of Representative Videos for Validation: Six videos were selected from the 228-video corpus by the research team (including 4 experienced early childhood clinicians) to represent the full range of interaction quality:
